title: microcerasus incana var. hippophaeoides
intro: Microcerasus incana var. hippophaeoides, also known as Sea Buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of flavonoids, carotenoids, and vitamins, which contribute to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Sea Buckthorn include improving cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of Sea Buckthorn include oil, juice, and tea, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
